name: SDN & Network Infringement
description: Infringement scoring overrides for SDN & Network Infrastructure patents. Adds networking-specific terminology mappings and necessary-implication guidance.
scoringGuidance: SDN and networking patents use highly abstract terminology. 'Network operating system' = 'controller', 'management platform', 'orchestrator'. 'Flow entries' = 'routing rules', 'forwarding policies', 'ACLs', 'route tables'. 'Managed switching elements' = 'switches', 'routers', 'network devices', 'nodes'.
Any product that centralizes network control necessarily maintains topology state, generates forwarding rules, and handles controller-to-device communication — even if undocumented.
Products implementing OpenFlow, NETCONF, or similar protocols necessarily practice many SDN architectural elements.
Cloud networking products (VPCs, WANs, SD-WAN) are inherently SDN implementations even if they don't use the term 'SDN'.
necessaryImplicationGuidance: Any SDN controller that manages network devices necessarily: (1) maintains a topology graph/database, (2) computes and installs forwarding rules, (3) handles controller-device communication protocols, (4) provides an API for policy expression. Any cloud networking product necessarily: (1) virtualizes network resources, (2) maintains isolation between tenants, (3) implements packet encapsulation/decapsulation.
